hypothesis: If open-weight language models are first supervised-fine-tuned on known BCC/B2 composition triplets and then optimized with DPO using preferences derived from Thermo-Calc hierarchical rewards, they will generate BCC/B2 alloy pairs that more frequently satisfy coexistence, BCC-first formation, room-temperature B2, and <10% impurity objectives—and achieve higher validity, coverage, novelty, and mean reward than baseline methods—because physics-based preference learning steers sampling toward thermodynamically favorable composition space.
